title: Geneseo (town), New York

infobox.value: [/ˌdʒɛnɪˈsiːoʊ/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") is a [town](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_New_York#Town "Administrative divisions of New York") in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") in the [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") region of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), United States. It is at the south end of the five-county [Rochester Metropolitan Area](/wiki/Rochester_metropolitan_area,_New_York "Rochester metropolitan area, New York"). The population of the town was 10,483 at the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_Census "2010 United States Census").[[4]](#cite_note-Census_2010-4)

Town in New York, United States

For the village located within this town, see [Geneseo (village), New York](/wiki/Geneseo_(village),_New_York "Geneseo (village), New York").

Not to be confused with [Genesee, New York](/wiki/Genesee,_New_York "Genesee, New York"); [Genesee County, New York](/wiki/Genesee_County,_New_York "Genesee County, New York"); or [Genesee Falls, New York](/wiki/Genesee_Falls,_New_York "Genesee Falls, New York").

**Geneseo** [/ˌdʒɛnɪˈsiːoʊ/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") is a [town](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_New_York#Town "Administrative divisions of New York") in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") in the [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") region of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), United States. It is at the south end of the five-county [Rochester Metropolitan Area](/wiki/Rochester_metropolitan_area,_New_York "Rochester metropolitan area, New York"). The population of the town was 10,483 at the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_Census "2010 United States Census").[[4]](#cite_note-Census_2010-4)

The English name "Geneseo" is an [anglicization](/wiki/Anglicization "Anglicization") of the [Seneca](/wiki/Seneca_language "Seneca language") name for the earlier Seneca town there, *Jo’néhsiyoh*, meaning "Good sand there."[[5]](#cite_note-5) The [village of Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(village),_New_York "Geneseo (village), New York") lies within the western portion of the town. The village and town are known today mainly as the home of the [State University of New York at Geneseo](/wiki/State_University_of_New_York_at_Geneseo "State University of New York at Geneseo").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geneseo,_New_York&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/New_York_-_Geneseo_-_NARA_-_68145089_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-New_York_-_Geneseo_-_NARA_-_68145089_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:New_York_-_Geneseo_-_NARA_-_68145089_(cropped).jpg)

Aerial view of Geneso in December 1923

### Pre-revolution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geneseo,_New_York&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pre-revolution")]

Near Geneseo was the largest[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] [Seneca](/wiki/Seneca_tribe "Seneca tribe") village, [Jo’néhsiyoh](/wiki/Little_Beard%27s_Town "Little Beard's Town"), a center of power for the [Haudenosaunee](/wiki/Haudenosaunee "Haudenosaunee") Confederacy. It was also the confederacy's "bread basket", with orchards, vineyards, and fields of maize and vegetables.

During the [American Revolution](/wiki/American_Revolution "American Revolution"), the Seneca joined the [British](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Great_Britain "Kingdom of Great Britain") and the [Tories](/wiki/Loyalist_(American_Revolution) "Loyalist (American Revolution)") against the colonists who were fighting for independence. The alliance's raids from the west were a major threat to the American cause, and [General Washington](/wiki/George_Washington "George Washington") sent the [Sullivan Expedition](/wiki/Sullivan_Expedition "Sullivan Expedition") to neutralize the Haudenosaunee. As Sullivan's army approached Geneseo with their "[scorched earth](/wiki/Scorched_earth "Scorched earth")" policy, the Senecas repeatedly fell back. However, a large Seneca party ambushed one of Sullivan's scouting parties, carried them as prisoners to Geneseo and tortured them to death. When Sullivan's troops arrived and found the savagely mutilated bodies, they became enraged and destroyed anything that could support the Haudenosaunee. No longer able to raid from Geneseo and the surrounding area, about 5,000 Seneca fled to British-held [Fort Niagara](/wiki/Fort_Niagara "Fort Niagara"), where they spent one of the coldest winters on record, with much loss of life, in camps outside the fort with only the small amount of supplies the British could spare.

### Post-revolution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geneseo,_New_York&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Post-revolution")]

The town was established in 1789, before the formation of Livingston County. The colonists' settlement of Geneseo began shortly after [James](/wiki/James_Wadsworth_(of_Geneseo) "James Wadsworth (of Geneseo)") and [William Wadsworth](/wiki/William_Wadsworth_(officer) "William Wadsworth (officer)") arrived in 1790. The brothers came to the [Genesee Valley](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River") from [Connecticut](/wiki/Connecticut "Connecticut") as agents of their uncle, Colonel [Jeremiah Wadsworth](/wiki/Jeremiah_Wadsworth "Jeremiah Wadsworth"), to care for and sell the land he purchased. The Wadsworths were part of the negotiations of the [Treaty of Big Tree](/wiki/Treaty_of_Big_Tree "Treaty of Big Tree") between [Robert Morris](/wiki/Robert_Morris_(merchant) "Robert Morris (merchant)") and the Senecas at the site of Geneseo in 1797.

Geneseo, as well as nearby [Mount Morris](/wiki/Mount_Morris,_New_York "Mount Morris, New York"), was part of the [Morris Reserve](/wiki/Morris_Reserve "Morris Reserve") that Morris held back from his sale of much of western New York to the [Holland Land Company](/wiki/Holland_Land_Company "Holland Land Company").

Geneseo was the birthplace of [Eliza Emily Chappell Porter](/wiki/Eliza_Emily_Chappell_Porter "Eliza Emily Chappell Porter") in 1807, a nurse, teacher, school builder, and [Underground Railroad](/wiki/Underground_Railroad "Underground Railroad") operative during the [Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War"). Geneseo was also the birthplace, in 1851, of the swindler [Ferdinand Ward](/wiki/Ferdinand_Ward "Ferdinand Ward").

Geneseo was a background for tales of the law and small town life by [Arthur C. Train](/wiki/Arthur_C._Train "Arthur C. Train"), who lived and practiced law in the town for many years before the First World War. He gave it the name of “Pottsville”, and often used it as one of the settings for his stories of the Yankee lawyer, Ephraim Tutt.

The village of Geneseo became the county seat of Livingston County in 1821 and was [incorporated](/wiki/Municipal_corporation "Municipal corporation") in 1832. The State Normal School, now [SUNY Geneseo](/wiki/State_University_of_New_York_at_Geneseo "State University of New York at Geneseo"), opened in 1871. In 1991, the [United States Department of the Interior](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_the_Interior "United States Department of the Interior") designated large parts of the village a [National Historic Landmark](/wiki/National_Historic_Landmark "National Historic Landmark").

The botanist [Albert Francis Blakeslee](/wiki/Albert_Francis_Blakeslee "Albert Francis Blakeslee") (1874–1954) was born in Geneseo.

### Present day

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geneseo,_New_York&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Present day")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/09/Doty_Building_at_SUNY_Geneseo.jpg/250px-Doty_Building_at_SUNY_Geneseo.jpg)](/wiki/File:Doty_Building_at_SUNY_Geneseo.jpg)

The Doty building, which was purchased and renovated for usage by SUNY Geneseo, was once Geneseo's high school. Currently it holds administrative offices for the college.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Livingston_County_Courthouse.jpg/250px-Livingston_County_Courthouse.jpg)](/wiki/File:Livingston_County_Courthouse.jpg)

The Livingston County Courthouse and offices are at the end of Main Street.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/P-51D_Mustang_%22Excalibur%22.jpg/250px-P-51D_Mustang_%22Excalibur%22.jpg)](/wiki/File:P-51D_Mustang_%22Excalibur%22.jpg)

A [P-51 Mustang](/wiki/P-51_Mustang "P-51 Mustang") at the 2007 Geneseo Airshow

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cd/Conesus_Lake_Ring_of_Fire_and_fireworks_2.jpg/250px-Conesus_Lake_Ring_of_Fire_and_fireworks_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Conesus_Lake_Ring_of_Fire_and_fireworks_2.jpg)

Conesus Lake during the 2006 "Ring of Fire"

The valley of the Genesee River is wide and fertile, with some of the best agricultural land in New York, but it was very prone to flooding, and Geneseo suffered several bad floods until the [United States Army Corps of Engineers](/wiki/United_States_Army_Corps_of_Engineers "United States Army Corps of Engineers")' construction of the [Mount Morris Dam](/wiki/Mount_Morris_Dam "Mount Morris Dam") upstream of the community in the 1950s.[[6]](#cite_note-around_geneseo-6) Agriculture is now a large contributor to Geneseo's economy. Geneseo is also used by many as a [bedroom community](/wiki/Bedroom_community "Bedroom community") for jobs in [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"), 30 miles (48 km) to the north. The village of Geneseo is governed by a mayor and four trustees.

The town can be roughly divided into three geographies: the village has a small-town atmosphere, much of Route 20A is commercialized, and the majority of the town's area is farmland. One of the main issues faced by the community today is [urban sprawl](/wiki/Urban_sprawl "Urban sprawl"). The increasing presence of [big-box stores](/wiki/Big-box_store "Big-box store") on Route 20A has been welcomed by some residents, who appreciate the convenience of nearby retailers, and discouraged by others, who oppose the suburbanization of the small town.

The Geneseo Airport (D52) is a general aviation airport west of the village, on the Wadsworth farm. It was established during July 1969, and is used for approximately 20 aircraft operations each day.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Since 1980, it has hosted groups restoring and operating historic military aircraft—originally the National Warplane Museum, and now the [1941 Historical Aircraft Group Museum](/wiki/1941_Historical_Aircraft_Group_Museum "1941 Historical Aircraft Group Museum"). An [airshow](/wiki/Airshow "Airshow") is held annually on the field, during the second weekend in July.

The Association for the Preservation of Geneseo (APOG) is a civic organization dedicated to preserving, improving, and restoring the places of civic, architectural, and historic interest to Geneseo and to educate members of the community to their architectural and historical heritage. Additional aims and purposes are to encourage others to contribute their knowledge, advice, and financial assistance.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[Sweet Briar](/wiki/Sweet_Briar_(Geneseo,_New_York) "Sweet Briar (Geneseo, New York)") and the [Wadsworth Fort Site](/wiki/Wadsworth_Fort_Site "Wadsworth Fort Site") are listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geneseo,_New_York&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Education")]

The [Geneseo Central School District](/wiki/Geneseo_Central_School_District "Geneseo Central School District") encompasses Geneseo and [Groveland](/wiki/Groveland,_New_York "Groveland, New York"), and consists of Geneseo Central School, which graduates approximately 75 students each year.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The school mascot is the Blue Devils and wear blue and white, with a gray accent color. Past accent colors included red and black.

In 1933, the school moved to its own building on School Street and in 1963 added a wing. In 1974 it moved into a new building at its present location on Avon Road.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

[SUNY Geneseo](/wiki/SUNY_Geneseo "SUNY Geneseo") is inside the village of Geneseo. There are approximately 5,000 undergraduate students.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geneseo,_New_York&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the town has an area of 45.1 square miles (116.9 km2), of which 43.9 square miles (113.8 km2) are land and 1.2 square miles (3.1 km2), or 2.65%, are water.[[12]](#cite_note-Gazetteer_2019-12)

The [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River") defines the western town line, and [Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake "Conesus Lake") is on part of the eastern town line. [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390_(New_York) "Interstate 390 (New York)") and [U.S. Route 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)") pass through the town, along with State Routes [39](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_39 "New York State Route 39"), [63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63"), and [256](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_256 "New York State Route 256").

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geneseo,_New_York&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Climate")]

Geneseo has a mild climate; summers typically bring temperatures between 60–80 °F (16–27 °C), while winters average 15–35 °F (−9–2 °C).

## Communities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geneseo,_New_York&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Communities")]

The town includes a number of [hamlets](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_New_York#Hamlet "Administrative divisions of New York") on the western shore of Conesus Lake. From north to south along NY 256 (West Lake Road), they are:[[14]](#cite_note-14)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/75/Conesus_Lake_panorama.jpg/960px-Conesus_Lake_panorama.jpg)](/wiki/File:Conesus_Lake_panorama.jpg)

Part of the east side of Conesus Lake as seen from Geneseo's Long Point Park

* Sleggs Landing
* Eagle Point
* Sacketts Harbor
* Wadsworth Cove
* Long Point
* Long Point Cove
* Cottonwood Cove

All of the listed hamlets are part of the larger [Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake,_New_York "Conesus Lake, New York") [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4d/Sturges_Hall_at_SUNY_Geneseo.jpg/250px-Sturges_Hall_at_SUNY_Geneseo.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sturges_Hall_at_SUNY_Geneseo.jpg)

Sturges Hall is SUNY Geneseo's landmark building.

As of the census[[2]](#cite_note-GR2-2) of 2000, there were 9,654 people, 2,523 households, and 1,303 families residing in the town. The population density was 219.6 inhabitants per square mile (84.8/km2). There were 2,698 housing units at an average density of 23.7 persons per square kilometre (61 persons/mi2). The town's racial makeup was 93.91% [White](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census"), 1.48% [African American](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census"), 0.11% [Native American](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census"), 2.61% [Asian](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census"), 0.04% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census"), 0.69% from [other races](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census"), and 1.15% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census") or [Latino](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census") residents of any race were 2.19% of the population.

There were 2,523 households, out of which 23.5% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 41.3% were married couples living together, 8.3% had a female householder with no husband present, and 48.4% were non-families. 26.0% of all households were made up of individuals, and 9.6% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.53 and the average family size was 2.92.

11.9% of the town's population was under age of 18, 51.3% was from age 18 to 24, 15.2% was from age 25 to 44, 13.2% was from age 45 to 64, and 8.5% were age 65 or older. The median age was 22 years. For every 100 females, there were 72.3 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 68.4 males.

The town's [median household income](/wiki/Median_income "Median income") was $40,660, and the median family income was $62,206. Males had a median income of $42,218 versus $25,969 for females. The town's per capita income was $15,303. About 8.7% of families and 29.8% of the population were below the poverty line, including 12.2% of those under the age of 18 and 5.5% ages 65 or older.
